AF74552347EBD0EACDDE4CD44D9A3F14: Entry/exit path inputs are specialised inputs that are located on the primary entry/exit path into the building. These create safe paths from the primary point of entry/exit to an LCD Terminal inside the area that allows area control. This allows for a brief period of time where the area can be entered while armed with no sirens sounding to give a chance to disarm, or armed without sirens sounding to give a chance to exit. Identify any inputs that are on the entry/exit path. For the input(s) that will be triggered first when entering the area, add them to the
B09354E6DA1A7303259FB2B7498BDB0A: For example, these outputs could be used to control lighting, secure indicator and alarm indicator within a section of the building, turning on if any units in that section are unlocked, secure and in alarm state.
B1D78777DAB97048C83C58060D49D168: - When the input is triggered, evacuation siren tones are played, strobes are enabled and an emergency event is sent to the monitoring station. The emergency response will occur regardless of the area's arm state. If tampered with, the same response will occur, except with a tamper event being sent to the monitoring station instead.
B219D2E2172BDBE54286FFFEC2F45E68: feature is used when a person is placed in a duress situation and is forced to login to an LCD terminal to control an area or output. If the Duress User has been enabled and a security PIN has been assigned, the user can login with this PIN instead of their own. Once logged in as the Duress User, the Duress input for the LCD Terminal will go into the alarm state, which will be reported to the monitoring station if alarm monitoring is configured without sounding any alarms. B23ABC56BBDB78BB462380A122CECB69: Allows an area to be associated with the lift floor. This area is the one used by the
B28CD12CAC7EA1388AC8CC5AA47D10AB: A custom output is an output that has no physical location or mechanism and exists only within the Inception Controller's logic. The custom output and the state that it represents can then be used to trigger Automated Actions. When programming automation, you may need to use a custom item to trigger some automation or to hold the state of some automation. This could be an output turning on when an input goes into alarm or needing something that is user controllable. It is not recommended to use a physical output as something could eventually be connected to the output or the relay may activate thousands of times a day.
B2B19A18008967B6D01C40110CF1381D: If enabled, a user badging a card to enter a door will automatically disarm the area on the opposite side of the door from where the user is located. The user will need to have the appropriate permissions to disarm the area. This maybe convenient as it removes the need to disarm the area from an LCD Terminal using the users PIN.
B2E539C25AAAF1C3781E2F3A24857A86: section. Once assigned, you will be able to select a process group to determine how the area will respond to the input state changes, this will determine the activation of sirens and the reporting of alarms. These different inputs may trigger alarms or sirens even if the area is disarmed.
B35A1ABB1E1CE047C8771EA206BF1FC2: A calculated input is an input that has no physical location or mechanism and exists only within the Inception Controller's logic. The calculated input and the state that it represents can be used in the system as a normal input, being added to areas and assigned an input type, triggering reportable alarm events and activating area sirens. With no physical hardware, the state of the input is instead controlled by the system.

B3A8BD3C4629D3ADE929DE31D3A97D71: Both options can be combined to result in a door that will only unlock when both the time period is valid and the area is disarmed. The door will relock when the time period goes invalid or the area re-arms. This allows a door to be normally unlocked during the day, but only after someone with authorization to disarm the area is there.
B4B14920755D0F2D4EE9D49340777BAE: Caution: this option could leave the area unsecure at an undesired time if time periods aren't managed properly. For example, the area could disarm on a public holiday when no one is around, leaving the area unprotected.
B4C144CFA29AF87CBA96DC63E46F721E: - Door Forced is a logical state for a door which occurs when a door is opened without the door being unlocked first. When this state occurs the door forced input will go into alarm. Once triggered, the Door Forced input will seal when the door closes or is unlocked.

B5F4688CFD7CDE76A416CFBE3375A635: A secure unit will enter the Alarm state if any of its sensors activate. Unlocked units can be opened without risk of alarm, but may enter an Unlocked Too Long state based on configuration. For units that will remain empty, they can be marked as vacant, allowing free access and no automation responses.
B74410CBA8CC6489C205DC9A4B56D51A: This option is used to determine the format that alarm messages will be reported with via the alarm communication unit. This should match the format that the alarm monitoring station is expecting.
B7BAC977FF4566C9DE18BE8DC60B1B5B: This page is used to enable, update or disable Service Mode. Service mode is used to disable area alarm processing, sirens, alarm reporting and door DOTL feedback during the initial setup and ongoing maintenance of the Inception system.

BA085BE04C373A229D80E0EA1CA2F966: Once the event limit is reached for an input or module health event, a separate event is sent to the monitoring station for this input or module to identify this. All new activations for that input or module health issue will then be discarded and will continue until an area disarm. For inputs, the limit is reset when the area that includes the input is disarmed. For module health events, as there is no area that they belong to, any area disarm will reset the limits.
BA431D844E63B633D24785A624247FAC: Determines what action or actions to perform when the trigger condition changes from true to false. These actions are edge-based and allow for the control of many things (outputs, areas, doors, inputs, readers, etc).
BA5B1865E4496A7F249C5D061D556AFC: input types) will not be sent. All other event types (including tamper events from intruder inputs) such as emergency, smoke, duress and environmental input alarms will still be reported. Commonly used when people will be on site near the armed area, such as a section of a building or if a downstairs area was armed at home. If an intruder event occurs, the sirens will sound to attract attention and allow investigation, but the monitoring station will not be notified.
BBC49FA4CA6FCBB33CF72E6402DB1920: - The T4000 is an Inner Range device that connects to the Inception Controller via USB. It provides multiple redundant reporting paths using multiple 3G networks in addition to ethernet internet reporting. With all paths polled frequently, this ensures reliable alarm monitoring.
BCEE8CF8B3DC1E383C13DB9B82279322: This trigger condition will be true if the number of users in the area meets the chosen comparison to the Number of Users value, and will turn back to false if it no longer meets this condition after the number of users in the area changes again.
BD15F678B79509CF0A97D77F77AC4A06: - An input with this behaviour lets the system know if the Unit's door is currently opened or closed. In addition, if the Unit is Secure, activation of this input will put the Unit into the Alarm state.
BD71AB9AA86B1934688462D3F703551C: - This will arm all areas currently configured in the system, that the user has permission to arm. Any areas that a user does not have permission to arm will not be armed. Exit delay will not be triggered.
BE0CA73071685F31E6E70D8F0A47126C: If enabled, this area can be armed into several different modes. This replaces the basic Arm mode, allowing the area to be armed in Full (Away), Perimeter (Stay) or Night (Instant) modes. These modes are commonly used in home scenarios, where the exterior of the building should be armed while people are inside. As with the standard arm mode, only the intruder related input types are affected. Inputs like Smoke and Duress will be monitored regardless of the arm mode.
BEDB3C24C05A2B199BBDD08999B94789: This trigger condition will be true when the selected Hardware Module has detected cabinet tampering, remaining true while the issue persists. This condition can apply to the Controller or any expansion module with a dedicated Cabinet Tamper input.

C0A8A05E077D1D1A79169454B7ED14F3: Allows the inside or outside area to control the door. When the selected area is armed, the door will lock, however when disarmed the door will not unlock. This feature can be used together with the
C0DA8CD9641014D236345B7ABB549830: drop-down selection. The area will automatically arm when the selected time period becomes active. By default, the area will not automatically disarm when the time period goes invalid, however this can be changed with the
C0F6B8BB24E5557B0A962C9B5FE562C1: Calculated inputs are created automatically under certain circumstances. For example, when a door is created in the system and is assigned a reed or tongue sense input, the Inception controller knows whether that door is open or closed. Two calculated inputs are automatically generated, one that represents when that door is forced (the door opened while locked) and one that represents when that door is open too long (held open past the held time limit and held warning time). These inputs can then be added to areas for monitoring and reporting those events.
C110C4DBFEB40E6E04E4692498AD3DE2: The current state of a test can be seen from multiple browsers with state dynamically updated. C3BA57FDF14B23437B163965C3004AE9: button, this will open all of the available siren devices. Only modules with the S1INT (internal) and S2EXT (external) outputs can drive 8 ohm horn sirens, for example, the 8 Zone LAN Expander. All Input Types (Process Groups) that are capable of triggering a siren will trigger both internal and external siren outputs.

C81C49DEB250781AF278906D24711D09: - This is the output that controls the electronic lock, when a door is unlocked the output will turn on. The door can be unlocked and locked by a user requesting access with a card or PIN, the web interface, or by pressing a REX (Request to Exit) button. The door can also be configured to automatically unlock and lock based on schedules or the state of the area on one side of the door.
C8923951BAA5DB29C4E8CF5792BFD213: Determines which menu is shown to the user after they log in to any LCD Terminal. This is only used for a normal login (PIN + OK), if the user logs in with an area control shortcut (PIN + ON to arm or PIN + OFF to disarm), they will be taken to the
C89778D55B4D3C9F5F7503C05C67E17A: The system tracks individual input events and module health events towards the repeat event limit, identifying each event separately. In this case, repeated alarm events from an input can trigger swinger shutdown and stopping future alarm events from that input from being sent, but if a tamper event is then detected on that input then it will still be reported. Similarly, a module's low battery event could be limited, but a cabinet tamper or AC fail event from that module is different and hence tracked and limited separately and will still be sent through.
C928106EF99C1DAB50D5961CE0FC6257: - Configures the input as a reader tamper input and once set you will need to link this input to a door. This input would normally be connected to the readers tamper wire to indicate when the reader has been tampered with. By using this behaviour, the module will monitor the reader for tampers and if detected, the module will report the alarm if alarm reporting is configured. This input should not be wired with EOL resistors and assumes that the tamper type is normally open. If the tamper type is actually normally closed, the
CA0EA1D2D30B47D579BB77CF26A5F452: The primary purpose of an intrusion detection system is to identify that an intruder has entered a building. The use of sirens, siren outputs and strobes are to ensure attention is drawn to the site and the intruder leaves the site quickly. In most cases, the intrusion detection is paired with alarm monitoring to report events to a monitoring station. The monitoring station will process the events according to their policies.
CAF50E8B0C0922A09C4A6899FFEB2A50: Determines which modules will activate the Siren S1INT and S2EXT outputs when an Input programmed to generate Sirens is triggered. Will also determine which Siren Outputs will activate for  the Area Arm Success, Area  Arm Fail  and Auto Arm warnings; as well as the Entry Delay and Exit Delay if siren activation is required.
CB906A72A5D8A180F8003DE67AE16312: Determines whether or not area alarm messages are displayed on the LCD Terminal when logged off. Will display the last alarm message that occurred in an area, including the input that triggered the alarm. Once someone has seen the message the user can press the

D01E7D108193674BB440D713C2E5D082: This page is used to easily create multiple areas.

D15CE1B75D3FB09F9CC811CEC5010221: If enabled, an input or module will only be able to trigger the sirens once for an area per arm cycle. Only the retriggering of sirens will be prevented, subsequent activations will still cause alarm events to be reported to a monitoring station if configured.
D2B37BD6F96DCDC2E9E820CB2E682190: - the status LED will turn red if the area is armed and will turn green when the area is disarmed. D2E755660F3BD3848823CF370AEA82E0: A commissioning report can be generated for either format via the toolbox above that can be sent to the monitoring station to be imported into their system. This provides detailed text descriptions of all possible events that can be reported by the system based on the the system configuration, along with the triggering event codes and point information. With this information, the monitoring station can convert the coded alarm events into clear and useful alarm information.
D330FF97A4EEFF5C8935DE2CDB7952C5: The entry/exit path is required when an LCD Terminal that allows area control is located within an armed area. This lets the terminal be protected, however a user still needs to be able to access the terminal to arm and disarm the area. A primary entry point can be designated which if triggered first, allows a user to move through the armed area to the LCD Terminal, forming the entry/exit path. The path is created using the security inputs, where the inputs that protect the path through the area are programmed differently. This lets a user enter through a specific door and walk a specific path to the terminal to disarm, or arm the area from the terminal and walk along the path to exit the area. Any deviation from this path causes an alarm to be triggered.
D356114F9A19556D8083DFE88D980BDB: In entry mode, an alarm will be triggered if the area is not disarmed before the entry time expires or if the user leaves the path and triggers an instant alarm input. Similarly, in exit mode, an alarm will be triggered if the user leaves the path and triggers an instant alarm input.
D45A62E64C4A9F52BBDF1BC9A0EAC78E: Temporary isolation occurs if the health issue was present when an area was armed, allowing the arm to succeed. This isolation will last until the area is disarmed. Users can also permenantly isolate modules from reporting issues if there is an ongoing fault with the module, removing the need to temporarily isolate the health issue each time the area is armed.
D4663DE1022A829BE4EB27116D00E447: The profile only determines which menu options the user can view and interact with via the LCD Terminal. D4B77629D6DC414F898003044741281E: - Any area disarms which cancel an active alarm in the area will be notified. The message will include which area was disarmed and who disarmed the area (either a user, an automated action or the system). It will also include that the disarm was to cancel an active alarm.
